findings.title: No test for muted+visualize rule producing signals
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test in test_backtest_costs.py or a new test_rule_signals.py that creates a StrategyRequest with a rule where muted=True and visualize=True. The viz_rules list is built by checking r.visualize with no muted guard, meaning a muted+visualized rule will still generate signal entries in rule_signals — even though eval_rule for a muted rule returns false in the main backtest loop. The current viz loop calls eval_rule directly (line 441) without checking r.muted, so signal output for a muted visualized rule depends entirely on eval_rule's own muted handling. Verify that behavior is intentional, then write a test that asserts the exact outcome.

findings.title: No test for visualized rule that fires on zero bars (empty signals list)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test where a rule with visualize=True never evaluates true across the bar range. The rule_signals list should still contain an entry for that rule (rule_index, label, side) with an empty signals array. This ensures the legend entry renders for the rule even when it did not fire. If rule_signals is only appended when signals is non-empty, that is an untested behavioral decision.

findings.title: rule_signals loop starts at i=1, skipping bar 0 — not tested
findings.suggested_fix: The viz loop iterates range(1, len(df)), meaning a signal that would fire on bar 0 is silently excluded. This is consistent with how eval_rules guards i < 1, but the omission is invisible to callers. Add a test confirming a 1-bar dataframe returns an empty signals list rather than raising an IndexError or including the bar-0 result.

findings.title: New visualize field on Rule not covered by any test in test_signal_engine.py
findings.suggested_fix: test_signal_engine.py has tests for every other Rule field (muted, negated, params, param, threshold) but no test for visualize default or round-trip serialization. Add: (1) test_rule_visualize_default_false that asserts Rule(indicator='rsi', condition='above', value=70).visualize == False, and (2) test_rule_visualize_true that constructs Rule(..., visualize=True) and asserts it survives model_dump/re-instantiation.

findings.title: Chart.tsx ruleSignals prop accepted but not exercised in chart-mount.test.tsx
findings.suggested_fix: renderChart() in chart-mount.test.tsx does not pass ruleSignals. Add a test case that passes a non-empty ruleSignals array (at least one buy-side and one sell-side entry with one signal each) and asserts the mount does not throw. This exercises the ruleSignalMarkers useMemo path and the createSeriesMarkers merge at line 611 with mixed trade + rule markers.

findings.title: Legend overlay rendered for rule_index >= RULE_SIGNAL_COLORS.length wraps via modulo — no test for wrap boundary
findings.suggested_fix: RULE_SIGNAL_COLORS has 8 entries. A rule_index of 8 maps to index 0 via modulo. No test verifies this wrap does not throw or produce undefined. Add a chart-mount test with ruleSignals containing rule_index: 8 (or higher) to ensure the color lookup does not crash.

findings.title: Eye icon toggle in RuleRow has no behavioral test
findings.suggested_fix: There is no RuleRow test file. The eye icon click fires onChange({ ...rule, visualize: !visualize }). A test should render RuleRow with visualize=false, simulate a click on the eye button, and assert onChange was called with visualize: true. Verify whether a RuleRow.test.tsx exists outside the diff before creating one.

residual_risks.description: The viz_rules loop re-runs eval_rule over the full bar range after the main backtest loop completes. For large date ranges with many visualized rules, this is an O(N*R) second pass. No test exists to catch a performance regression if this grows. Not a correctness issue but a risk if rule counts scale.

rule_signals is only included in the response JSON when the list is non-empty (line 484). If the frontend expects the key to always be present when visualize=True rules exist but all had empty signals, the conditional omission could cause a frontend undefined-access. No contract test covers this absence.

testing_gaps.area: Backend: per-rule signal collection logic (backtest.py lines 430-451)
testing_gaps.gap: Zero tests for any behavior of the new viz_rules collection and signal loop. Edge cases with no visualized rules, muted+visualized rules, rules that never fire, and single-bar datasets are all uncovered.
test_signal_engine.py tests all other Rule fields but has no test for visualize default or serialization.
chart-mount.test.tsx never passes ruleSignals. The ruleSignalMarkers memo, the createSeriesMarkers merge, and the legend overlay DOM branch are all untested.
No test file exists for RuleRow. The visualize toggle is pure UI state with an onChange contract — an easy unit test with @testing-library/react.
